title: The Poisson distribution
summary: 9709 S2 — Poisson model, probabilities, mean λ, and binomial approximation with GeoGebra.
learningObjectives: Identify situations suitable for Poisson model.
Use the property E(X) = Var(X) = λ.

simpleExplanation.title: Rare events in an interval
simpleExplanation.summary: Poisson counts events in fixed time/space when events are rare and independent. Mean and variance both equal λ.
steps: X ~ Po(λ): P(X = r) = e^{−λ} λ^r / r!.
Binomial B(n,p) ≈ Po(np) when n large, p small.
Use cumulative tables for P(X ≤ k).

sections.content: **Poisson** (6.1) opens Statistics 2. Justify **λ** from context (e.g. average calls per hour) before calculating.
